question: How many PTO days do I get after 4 years of service?
answer: After 4 years of service, you receive 20 PTO days annually [Source: 01_PTO_Policy.docx, Section 3. Accrual Rates].
expected_answer: 20 days per year (1.67 days/month accrual), based on the 3-5 years of service tier.

question: Can I carry over unused PTO into next year?
answer: Yes, employees may carry over up to 5 unused PTO days into the next calendar year. Any unused balance beyond 5 days is forfeited on December 31, unless required otherwise by state law [Source: 01_PTO_Policy.docx, Section 4. Carryover].
expected_answer: Yes, up to 5 unused PTO days may be carried over. Anything beyond that is forfeited on Dec 31, unless state law requires otherwise.

question: Do Directors accrue PTO the same way as other employees?
answer: No, Directors do not accrue PTO in the same way as other employees. They are enrolled in the Flexible PTO program instead of the accrual-based PTO system [Source: 01_PTO_Policy.docx, Section 8. Unlimited PTO Exception].
expected_answer: No, Directors and above are on Flexible PTO with no accrual, no payout, but a minimum expectation of 15 days off per year.

question: What happens to my unused PTO if I quit?
answer: Upon voluntary or involuntary termination, unused accrued PTO is paid out at the employee’s current base rate, in accordance with state law [Source: 01_PTO_Policy.docx, Section 7. PTO Payout].
expected_answer: Unused accrued PTO is paid out at the employee's current base rate upon termination, per state law.

question: When does my health insurance coverage start as a new employee?
answer: Your health insurance coverage starts on the first day of the month following 30 days of employment [Source: 02_Health_Benefits_Policy.pdf, Section 2. Eligibility & Enrollment].
expected_answer: Coverage begins on the first day of the month following 30 days of employment.

question: Does the company contribute to my HSA?
answer: Yes, the company contributes $1,000/year to the HSA for employees enrolled in Plan B (High-Deductible Plan) [Source: 02_Health_Benefits_Policy.pdf, Section 3. Medical Plans].
expected_answer: Yes, $1,000/year for employees enrolled in the High-Deductible Plan B (HSA-eligible).

question: How much is the vision allowance for frames or contacts?
answer: The vision allowance for frames or contacts is $150 [Source: 02_Health_Benefits_Policy.pdf, Section 4. Dental & Vision].
expected_answer: $150 allowance toward frames/lenses or contacts, plus a fully covered annual eye exam.

question: Up to what age can I keep my child on my health plan?
answer: You can keep your child on your health plan up to age 26 [Source: 02_Health_Benefits_Policy.pdf, Section 5. Dependent Coverage].
expected_answer: Dependent children can be covered up to age 26.

question: How many sessions does the EAP cover?
answer: The Employee Assistance Program (EAP) covers up to 6 sessions per year per issue [Source: 02_Health_Benefits_Policy.pdf, Section 7. Wellness Program].
expected_answer: Up to 6 free confidential counseling sessions per year, per issue.

question: How many weeks of paid leave does a birthing parent get?
answer: A birthing parent gets 12 weeks of fully paid leave [Source: 03_Leave_of_Absence_Policy.docx, Section 3. Parental Leave (Company-Paid, Supplemental to FMLA)].
expected_answer: 12 weeks fully paid, running concurrently with FMLA where applicable.

question: Am I eligible for FMLA if I've only worked here 6 months?
answer: No, you are not eligible for FMLA if you have only worked here for 6 months, as eligibility requires at least 12 months of service [Source: 03_Leave_of_Absence_Policy.docx, Section 2. Family and Medical Leave (FMLA)].
expected_answer: No, FMLA requires 12+ months of service and 1,250+ hours worked in the prior 12 months, at a location with 50+ employees within 75 miles.

question: How many paid bereavement days do I get for losing a grandparent?
answer: You get 2 paid days for the loss of a grandparent [Source: 03_Leave_of_Absence_Policy.docx, Section 5. Bereavement Leave].
expected_answer: 2 paid days, since a grandparent is classified as extended family.

question: Is my job protected if I take jury duty leave?
answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.
expected_answer: Full pay is continued for up to 10 business days with court documentation; additional unpaid leave is available if service extends longer.

question: How often do I need to check in with HR while on leave?
answer: Employees on approved leave should check in with HR at least every 30 days regarding return-to-work status [Source: 03_Leave_of_Absence_Policy.docx, Section 9. Requesting Leave].
expected_answer: At least every 30 days regarding return-to-work status.

question: How many days a week do hybrid employees need to be in office?
answer: Hybrid employees need to be in the office a minimum of 2 days per week, specifically on core days which are Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday [Source: 04_Remote_Work_Policy.pdf, Section 2. Work Location Categories].
expected_answer: Minimum 2 days/week, on the designated core days (Tue/Wed/Thu).

question: What is the home office stipend for new fully remote hires?
answer: The one-time home office setup stipend for new fully-remote hires is $300 [Source: 04_Remote_Work_Policy.pdf, Section 4. Equipment & Reimbursement].
expected_answer: A one-time $300 home office setup stipend, plus $50/month internet reimbursement.

question: Can I work from another country for a month without approval?
answer: No, you cannot work from another country for a month without approval. International remote work requires 30 days’ advance notice and Legal/HR sign-off, and you can only work a maximum of 20 business days per year without a formal international employment agreement [Source: 04_Remote_Work_Policy.pdf, Section 7. Out-of-State / International Remote Work].
expected_answer: No, international remote work requires 30 days' advance notice and Legal/HR sign-off, with a max of 20 business days/year without a formal international employment agreement.

question: What are the core collaboration hours I need to be available for?
answer: You need to be available during core collaboration hours from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in your assigned time zone [Source: 04_Remote_Work_Policy.pdf, Section 5. Work Hours & Availability].
expected_answer: 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in the employee's assigned time zone.

question: What is the employer 401k match if I contribute 5% of my salary?
answer: If you contribute 5% of your salary, the employer will match 100% of the first 3% and 50% of the next 2%. This results in a total employer match of 4% of your salary, as there is no additional match beyond 5% [Source: 05_Retirement_401k_Policy.docx, Section 4. Employer Matching].
expected_answer: 100% match on the first 3%, plus 50% match on the next 2%, totaling a 4% employer match.

question: Am I fully vested in employer match after 2 years?
answer: No, after 2 years you are 50% vested in employer matching contributions, not fully vested. Full vesting occurs after 4 years of service [Source: 05_Retirement_401k_Policy.docx, Section 5. Vesting Schedule].
expected_answer: No, only 50% vested after 2 years. Full vesting (100%) occurs at 4+ years of service.

question: What's the maximum 401k loan I can take?
answer: The maximum 401(k) loan you can take is up to 50% of your vested balance, with a maximum limit of $50,000 [Source: 05_Retirement_401k_Policy.docx, Section 7. Loans & Hardship Withdrawals].
expected_answer: Up to 50% of vested balance, capped at $50,000, repaid via payroll deduction over up to 5 years.

question: When am I automatically enrolled in the 401k plan and at what rate?
answer: You are automatically enrolled in the 401k plan on the first payroll cycle following 90 days of employment and reaching age 21, at a rate of 4% of your salary (pre-tax) unless you opt out or change the contribution rate within 30 days [Source: 05_Retirement_401k_Policy.docx, Section 2. Eligibility & Enrollment].
expected_answer: Auto-enrollment at 4% of salary (pre-tax) on the first payroll cycle after 90 days of employment and reaching age 21, unless the employee opts out or changes the rate.

question: Does the company offer a signing bonus for new hires?
answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.
expected_answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.

question: What is the company's policy on stock options and equity grants?
answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.
expected_answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.

question: What is the process for filing an expense reimbursement report?
answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.
expected_answer: Not covered in the available policy documents.
